questions.question: A doctor prescribes $2$ mills of a medication to be taken $3$ times a day. What is the daily dosage rate in mills per day?
The population of a certain bacteria colony grows at a rate of $25%$ per hour. If there are initially $100$ bacteria, how many will there be after $2$ hours?
A patient is prescribed a medication that must be taken at a rate of $15$ml per $30$ minutes. What is the hourly rate of medication in ml/hour?

The rate of cell division in a certain organism is directly proportional to the concentration of a certain nutrient. If $100$ cells divide in $2$ hours when the nutrient concentration is $5$mg/L, how many cells will divide in $3$ hours when the concentration is $7.5$mg/L?
A certain medical treatment requires a dosage of $2$mg of medication per $10$kg of body weight. What is the total dosage required for a patient weighing $60$kg?

The rate at which a certain enzyme catalyzes a reaction is inversely proportional to the concentration of a certain inhibitor. If $50$ units of the enzyme are required to catalyze the reaction in $10$ minutes when the inhibitor concentration is $2$mg/L, how many units of the enzyme will be required when the concentration is $5$mg/L?
A certain medication has a $20%$ absorption rate when taken orally. If $100$mg of the medication are taken, how much will actually be absorbed into the bloodstream?
A patient is prescribed a daily dosage of $50$mg of a certain medication, which must be taken in $5$ equal doses throughout the day. What is the dosage rate per dose in mg/dose?

A certain type of cancer cell grows at a rate that is directly proportional to the concentration of a certain protein in the bloodstream. If $500$ cancer cells are present when the protein concentration is $10$mg/L, and $2000$ cancer cells are present after $2$ hours, what is the rate of growth of the cancer cells in cells/hour? Show all work and explain your reasoning. Assume the protein concentration remains constant.

A doctor is conducting an experiment to determine the effect of a certain medication on blood pressure. The doctor measures the blood pressure of $10$ patients before and after taking the medication, and records the results in the table below. Using the data, calculate the average rate of change of blood pressure in mmHg/hour and explain what this value represents in the context of the experiment.

teacher_rules: When solving problems involving rates and variation, be sure to identify the variables and constants in the problem.
Use units and dimensional analysis to ensure that your answers have the correct units and make sense in the context of the problem.
